title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for player outcomes, repository baseline, accessibility, and evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
Order the Player Outcome Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Player Outcome.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Player Outcome assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Player Outcome, keep Player Outcome connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 7585 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Evidence Ladder, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Evidence Ladder precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Evidence Ladder, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 7428 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Evidence Ladder, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible.

stimulus.title: Evidence Ladder failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 9047 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Player Outcome to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Player Outcome.

checks.description: The decision applies Acceptance Evidence to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Acceptance Evidence.
